1	# Tool Registry Foundation TechSpec
2	
3	## Executive Summary
4	
5	This TechSpec designs AGH's Tool Registry as a daemon-owned runtime service, not as a static list of built-in commands. The registry will unify tool identity, discovery, availability, policy, execution, hooks, telemetry, extension descriptors, MCP adapters, and session-visible exposure through one central dispatch pipeline.
6	
7	There is no existing `_prd.md` for this task. The scope is based on the user request, competitor research under `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/`, accepted ADRs under `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/adrs/`, current AGH code exploration, and the prior autonomous skills/tools registry gap analysis.
8	
9	The primary architectural trade-off is to make AGH-native tools visible to sessions through an AGH-hosted local MCP server in the MVP instead of trying to invent an ACP-specific registry. ACP does not define a callable tool registry; it defines session lifecycle, MCP bootstrap, permission callbacks, and tool-call observations. MCP provides the programmatic `Tool.name`, so AGH will expose its canonical `ToolID` directly as the hosted MCP tool name.
10	
11	The foundation will support executable native/bundled tools, executable extension-host tools, and executable MCP-backed tools. Built-in AGH tools execute in-process through `native_go` handles compiled into the daemon. Third-party TypeScript and Go extension tools execute out-of-process through the existing extension runtime, a new `tool.provider` capability, `provide_tools` reconciliation, and `tools/call` RPC. MCP-backed tools execute through daemon-owned MCP clients that consume the existing MCP config/auth subsystem. Descriptor-only is an unavailable/error state, not the MVP contract for extension or MCP tools.
12	
13	Implementation direction: AGH should use the official Go SDK `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` for MCP protocol/session/transport behavior instead of planning hand-rolled MCP JSON-RPC, stdio, or streamable-HTTP plumbing inside the Tool Registry workstream. AGH still owns registry policy, canonical `ToolID`, approval bridging, UDS/session binding, provenance, redaction, and durable MCP auth state.
14	
15	## MVP Boundary Statement
16	
17	MVP boundary: implementation steps 1-16 build the Tool Registry foundation, AGH-hosted MCP session exposure, native bootstrap tools, executable TypeScript/Go extension-host tools, executable daemon-owned MCP call-through, shared CLI/HTTP/UDS surfaces, policy/availability enforcement, hooks, observability, docs, SDK updates, and verification. This MVP proves the registry as an executable daemon primitive without replacing every ACP provider-native tool.
18	
19	Post-MVP work deferred to later TechSpecs:
20	
21	- direct driver-specific tool injection outside hosted MCP;
22	- full shell/browser/file tool replacement for ACP runtimes;
23	- remote peer tool execution over AGH Network;
24	- provider-specific deferred schema loading such as Anthropic `tool_reference`;
25	- broad marketplace signing/trust overhaul;
26	- skill install/remove/update tools;
27	- bridge SDK executable tool adapters;
28	- direct in-process plugin loading for third-party Go or TypeScript code;
29	- client-supplied ACP `mcpServers` as session-scoped registry sources.
30	
31	Explicitly out of scope for this TechSpec:
32	
33	- in-process third-party extension handlers;
34	- silent compatibility aliases for dotted tool IDs;
35	- policy bypasses for `approve-all`;
36	- partial surface delivery where CLI/HTTP ships without UDS, docs, codegen, and hosted MCP parity;
37	- storing matchable ownership or authorization state inside opaque JSON metadata blobs.
38	
39	Backend delivery boundary:
40	
41	| Backend kind | MVP delivery | Invocation behavior |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| `native_go` | Descriptor, availability, policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable in-process only for daemon-compiled AGH built-ins |
44	| `extension_host` | Manifest-authoritative descriptor, runtime reconciliation, extension health, source policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable out-of-process through existing extension subprocess runtime, `tool.provider`, and `tools/call`; TypeScript and Go SDKs wrap handlers as functions |
45	| `mcp` | Descriptor discovery, source provenance, health/auth diagnostics, collision handling, source policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable through daemon-owned MCP clients using existing MCP config and `internal/mcp/auth` status/token interfaces |
46	| `subprocess` | Not a public registry backend kind in MVP | Rejected by manifest validation; third-party Go/TypeScript tools use `extension_host`, whose implementation is subprocess-isolated |
47	| `bridge` | Reserved post-MVP backend kind | Rejected by MVP validation unless a later bridge TechSpec enables it |
48	
49	AGH-hosted MCP is different from `mcp` backend tools. Hosted MCP is the session exposure transport for AGH registry tools; `mcp` backend tools are external tools contributed by MCP servers and are executable only through daemon-owned MCP client adapters after the same registry policy, source, approval, hook, and session-lineage gates pass.
50	
51	## Architectural Boundaries
52	
53	`internal/daemon` remains the only composition root. It may import and compose `internal/tools`, `internal/skills`, `internal/mcp`, `internal/extension`, `internal/hooks`, `internal/session`, `internal/network`, `internal/task`, `internal/api/*`, and config/resource stores. No package may import `internal/daemon`.
54	
55	Package import boundaries:
56	
57	- `internal/tools` owns `ToolID`, descriptors, backend kinds, providers, handles, registry, policy interfaces, availability, dispatch contracts, and result normalization. It must not import `internal/daemon`, `internal/api/*`, `internal/cli`, `internal/extension`, `internal/session`, `internal/network`, or `internal/task`.
58	- `internal/catalog`, if added, is a thin composition-facing facade over `internal/tools` and `internal/skills`. It must not own tool dispatch or policy.
59	- `internal/extension` may publish manifest-authoritative tool descriptors and expose live out-of-process extension tool invokers through public registry contracts. It must not execute third-party tool handlers in-process and must not import registry internals beyond public `internal/tools` descriptor/provider contracts.
60	- `internal/mcp` may adapt external MCP tools, call external MCP servers through daemon-owned clients, and host the AGH MCP stdio proxy. It wraps the official Go MCP SDK behind AGH-owned interfaces and must keep all AGH-owned calls entering `internal/tools.Registry.Call` through UDS or an injected interface; MCP code must not duplicate dispatch policy.
61	- `internal/mcp/auth` already owns remote MCP OAuth 2.1 + PKCE, redacted status, token refresh/logout, and durable token storage through `internal/store/globaldb`. The Tool Registry may consume redacted auth status through a daemon-injected interface, but it must not reimplement OAuth flows, open the MCP auth token store directly, or persist remote MCP token material.
62	- `internal/api/core` owns transport-independent handlers. `internal/api/httpapi` and `internal/api/udsapi` only register routes and transport concerns.
63	- `internal/cli` calls UDS/HTTP client methods and does not import runtime registry implementations.
64	- `internal/hooks` owns typed hook payloads and execution. Hooks dispatch at the registry call site; no code may tail event tables to trigger tool hooks.
65	- `internal/store` may persist session lineage permission atoms and events. It must not decide tool policy; it validates and stores normalized atoms.
66	- `internal/session` can receive hosted MCP server config and session projections through interfaces. It must not implement a parallel tool registry.
67	
68	Boundaries to update in implementation:
69	
70	- If `internal/catalog` or a new `internal/mcp` subpackage is added, update `magefile.go` package boundary checks in the same change.
71	- Any OpenAPI/contract change must co-ship generated `openapi/agh.json` and `web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts`.
72	- Any CLI surface must co-ship HTTP/UDS parity unless the spec explicitly marks the surface CLI-only. This TechSpec has no CLI-only tool surfaces.
73	
74	## System Architecture
75	
76	### Component Overview
77	
78	| Component | Responsibility | Boundary |
79	|---|---|---|
80	| `internal/tools` runtime registry | Owns canonical `ToolID`, descriptors, backend kinds, providers, handles, availability projection, policy evaluation, dispatch, result normalization, and telemetry hooks | Does not import `daemon`, `api`, or `cli`; composed by `internal/daemon` |
81	| Cold `tool` resources | Persist desired-state tool metadata from extensions, bundles, and future dynamic producers | Metadata only; no function pointers or execution handles |
82	| Built-in `native_go` provider | Registers AGH-native tools such as tool search, skill view, network peers/send, and bounded task tools | In-process only because it ships inside the daemon binary |
83	| Extension-host provider | Converts extension-published tool resources into runtime descriptors, reconciles them with `provide_tools`, and invokes handlers over extension subprocess RPC | Does not execute extension code in-process; fails closed on manifest/runtime mismatch |
84	| Extension SDKs | TypeScript and Go helper APIs let extension authors define tools with functions while the runtime remains out-of-process | SDKs generate/reconcile manifest descriptors and register `tools/call` handlers |
85	| MCP adapter provider | Normalizes MCP-backed descriptors, health, auth status, source provenance, and executes calls through daemon-owned MCP clients wrapping the official Go MCP SDK | Fails closed on health, auth, policy, approval, hook, or name collision problems |
86	| Existing MCP auth runtime | Supplies redacted remote MCP OAuth 2.1 + PKCE status for external MCP diagnostics | Owned by `internal/mcp/auth`; registry must not duplicate OAuth flow, token storage, or token refresh/logout |
87	| `internal/catalog` facade | Thin cross-domain list/search/view facade over tools and skills | Optional coordination layer; runtime tool dispatch remains in `internal/tools` |
88	| Policy engine | Combines ACP approval mode, session lineage, agent policy, source/risk defaults, registry allow/deny, toolsets, availability, and hooks | Produces structured effective decisions, never a single ambiguous boolean |
89	| AGH-hosted MCP proxy | Exposes session-callable AGH tools as MCP tools using canonical `ToolID` names | Runs through daemon-provided `agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce>`, uses the official Go MCP SDK server/tool APIs over stdio, and proxies to daemon over UDS |
90	| API/CLI surfaces | Expose machine-readable list/search/info/invoke/status behavior | Shared contracts in `internal/api/contract`, handlers in `internal/api/core`, HTTP/UDS parity |
91	| Hook integration | Runs `tool.pre_call`, `tool.post_call`, and `tool.post_error` around registry dispatch | Hooks can deny, narrow, patch, redact, or annotate, but cannot bypass policy |
92	| Observability | Emits durable events and metrics for registration, projection, decisions, calls, failures, conflicts, truncation, and policy denials | Redacts secrets and raw tokens |
93	
94	Data flow:
95	
96	1. Extensions, built-ins, MCP servers, and future dynamic providers register cold descriptors and, where executable, runtime handles.
97	2. `internal/daemon` composes providers into `internal/tools.Registry`.
98	3. Registry indexes descriptors by canonical `ToolID`.
99	4. Operator surfaces can list all tools with status and reason codes.
100	5. Session/model-visible surfaces request a session projection and receive only callable tools for that effective context.
101	6. Every invocation enters `Registry.Call`, which validates schema, recomputes availability/policy, runs hooks, calls the `native_go`, `extension_host`, or `mcp` handle, normalizes output, persists/observes, and returns a bounded result.
102	
103	## Implementation Design
104	
105	### Core Interfaces
106	
107	These are final-shape interface contracts for implementation planning. Implementers may add helper types, but registry dispatch must preserve these method responsibilities and must not reintroduce metadata-only runtime providers.
108

112		Search(ctx context.Context, scope Scope, q SearchQuery) ([]ToolView, error)

114		Call(ctx context.Context, scope Scope, req CallRequest) (ToolResult, error)
115	}

162	
163	`MCPCallExecutor` is implemented by `internal/mcp`, not by `internal/tools`. It resolves bearer material through `internal/mcp/auth` internally, never exposes `mcpauth.TokenRecord` or raw headers to registry code, and returns only normalized `ToolResult` plus wrapped backend errors.
164	
165	### MCP SDK Adoption
166	
167	`internal/mcp` wraps `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` for MCP protocol/session/transport behavior. This is a protocol implementation choice, not a policy boundary change.
168	
169	- MVP validation is pinned to `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.5.0`. Any SDK version upgrade requires fresh focused tests plus TechSpec/ADR review before adoption.
170	- Primary-source anchor points for this decision:
171	  - `auth/client.go` defines the `auth.OAuthHandler` `TokenSource` / `Authorize` contract that streamable HTTP transports call on 401/403.
172	  - `mcp/protocol.go` defines `Tool.InputSchema` / `Tool.OutputSchema` as explicit schema-bearing fields and `mcp/tool.go` defines the broader SDK tool-name grammar.
173	  - `mcp/streamable.go` defines `StreamableClientTransport`, `DisableStandaloneSSE`, and `EventStore`.
174	- Hosted AGH MCP proxy uses `mcp.NewServer`, SDK `Tool` definitions, and `mcp.StdioTransport`. AGH session projections still decide which tools are registered, and every tool call still re-enters `Registry.Call` through AGH-owned UDS/session binding.
175	- External stdio MCP servers use `mcp.NewClient` plus `mcp.CommandTransport`.
176	- External remote HTTP MCP servers use `mcp.NewClient` plus `mcp.StreamableClientTransport{DisableStandaloneSSE: true}`.
177	- Hosted MCP tool registration MUST construct SDK `*mcp.Tool` values with `InputSchema` and `OutputSchema` taken byte-for-byte from `Descriptor.input_schema` and `Descriptor.output_schema`. SDK reflection-based schema helpers are forbidden for AGH-hosted tools because AGH manifest/runtime digests treat descriptor schema bytes as authoritative.
178	- If remote streamable MCP auth is integrated with SDK transport hooks, AGH must provide an adapter implementing the SDK `auth.OAuthHandler` interface backed by `internal/mcp/auth`; AGH must not replace its durable token store or redacted status lifecycle with SDK defaults.
179	- AGH MUST NOT use SDK `auth.AuthorizationCodeHandler`, `auth/authorization_code.go`, or any SDK-owned token cache/refresh flow as the authority for remote MCP credentials. Login, refresh, logout, and durable token state remain `internal/mcp/auth` plus existing `agh mcp auth ...` surfaces.
180	- MVP does not configure an SDK `EventStore` for remote MCP client transports. Remote MCP sessions are on-demand, bounded by executor idle TTL, and do not implement resumability/redelivery in this workstream.
181	- Legacy AGH config value `mcp_server.transport = "sse"` is removed by this TechSpec. Post-change, the only valid remote MCP transport value is `http`, which means SDK-backed streamable HTTP. AGH must not silently reinterpret persisted `sse` as `http`.
182	- AGH keeps its stricter canonical `ToolID` grammar even though the SDK accepts a broader tool-name grammar. AGH `ToolID` remains a valid subset and is passed to the SDK as MCP `Tool.name`.
183	- Hosted MCP tool registration should use SDK list-change behavior rather than inventing a parallel MCP notification format. AGH still owns when a projection changes and which session is allowed to observe it.
184	
185	OAuth handler adapter contract:
186	
187	- `TokenSource(ctx)` is implemented inside `internal/mcp` and reads the current usable server credential only through AGH-owned MCP auth boundaries. It may construct an in-memory token source for the outbound SDK transport, but raw access/refresh tokens never leave `internal/mcp`.
188	- `Authorize(ctx, req, resp)` is the only SDK callback allowed to react to a remote `401` or `403`. It must close `resp.Body`, inspect AGH's persisted auth status for the target server, and choose one of two behaviors only:
189	  - if the persisted state is `authenticated` or `expired` and the token is refreshable, attempt exactly one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` and return `nil` only when that refresh succeeds;
190	  - otherwise return a redacted backend error mapped to `mcp_auth_required`, `mcp_auth_expired`, `mcp_auth_invalid`, or `mcp_auth_refresh_failed`.
191	- `Authorize` MUST NOT start a new authorization-code flow, open a browser, prompt the operator, or create any credential path outside `agh mcp auth login`.
192	- Any token write caused by `Authorize` goes through the existing `internal/mcp/auth` token store only. No SDK-owned token cache, sidecar file, memory cache, or duplicate persistence layer is allowed.

300	### Data Models
301	
302	`ToolID`
303	
304	- Canonical public ID used by registry, policy, CLI, HTTP, UDS, hooks, telemetry, and hosted MCP.
305	- Format: `<segment>( "__" <segment> )*`
306	- Segment regex: `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*`
307	- Max length: 64.
308	- Lowercase ASCII only.
309	- `__` is reserved as namespace separator.
310	- No dots, hyphens, uppercase, empty segments, or dual wire aliases.
311	- External MCP/extension names that sanitize to more than 64 characters are rejected as conflicted with `id_too_long`; AGH does not truncate, hash-suffix, or create shadow aliases.
312

368	
369	Schema digest contract:
370	
371	- `input_schema_digest` and `output_schema_digest` are lowercase hex `sha256` digests over the JSON Schema subtree only.
372	- The bytes hashed are RFC 8785 JCS-canonicalized JSON. Object keys are sorted by the canonicalization algorithm, number/string escaping follows JCS, and `$ref` values are hashed literally rather than resolved during digesting.
373	- The digest excludes surrounding manifest metadata such as `ToolID`, handler, source, risk, and toolsets.
374	- TypeScript SDK, Go SDK, and daemon manifest validation must share byte-vector fixtures under `sdk/typescript/test-fixtures/digest/`, `sdk/go/test-fixtures/digest/`, and `internal/extension/testdata/digest/`.
375	- A digest mismatch is a hard `extension_runtime_mismatch`. There is no loose fallback, serializer-specific fallback, or warning-only mode.
376	
377	`MCPToolDescriptor`
378	
379	- `raw_name string`
380	- `title string`
381	- `description string`
382	- `input_schema json.RawMessage`
383	- `output_schema json.RawMessage`
384	- `source SourceRef`
385	
386	`MCPToolDescriptor` is the daemon-internal normalized projection of one upstream MCP `Tool` before registry `Descriptor` synthesis. `input_schema` and `output_schema` preserve upstream schema bytes as received; AGH does not infer, regenerate, or drop them during MCP normalization.

436	
437	MCP-backed descriptors may attach a redacted `MCPAuthStatus` to operator-visible tool views only. The status mirrors the existing `internal/mcp/auth.StatusValue` values (`unconfigured`, `needs_login`, `authenticated`, `expired`, `invalid`) plus registry reason codes, and may include `server_name`, `auth_type`, `client_id`, `scopes`, `expires_at`, `refreshable`, `token_present`, and `diagnostic`. It must never include access tokens, refresh tokens, OAuth authorization codes, PKCE verifiers, client secrets, approval tokens, or hosted MCP bind nonces. Session/model-visible projections do not include `MCPAuthStatus`; they hide or deny the tool through `Availability` reason codes.
438	
439	`EffectiveToolDecision`

466	`Toolset`
467	
468	Toolsets are separate named resources/config entries, not overloaded as tools. They use a typed `ToolsetID`, include exact `ToolID`s and patterns, and may include other toolsets recursively. Policy fields must distinguish `tools` and `toolsets` to avoid ambiguity.
469	
470	`ToolsetID` uses the same grammar as `ToolID`: lowercase ASCII segments separated by reserved `__`, maximum 64 characters, and no dots, hyphens, uppercase, or empty segments.
471	
472	`dynamic` source kind is reserved because the existing source enum already has it, but MVP has no dynamic producer and no dynamic validation surface. Dynamic tools remain unavailable unless a later TechSpec defines their producer, policy, and provenance model.

474	### Data-Model Field Rationale

477	|---|---|---|---|

479	| `Descriptor.backend` | structured `BackendRef` | Binds the descriptor to the only allowed executable backend path | Typed struct; dispatch never infers backend from source prefix or metadata |

482	| `Descriptor.output_schema` | JSON Schema object | Optional structured output contract | JSON schema payload is appropriate for the same reason as input schema |
483	| `Descriptor.visibility` | enum | Separate internal/operator/session/model surfaces | Typed enum, queryable and policy-relevant |
484	| `Descriptor.risk` | enum | Classify read/mutate/network/open-world behavior | Typed enum, never free-text metadata |
485	| `Descriptor.read_only` | bool | Drives `approve-reads` and safety checks | Typed bool; misclassification is a security bug |
486	| `Descriptor.destructive` | bool | Forces stricter policy and approval behavior | Typed bool, not metadata |

489	| `Descriptor.max_result_bytes` | int64 | Enforces result budget consistently across surfaces | Typed numeric field with config default |

506	No new SQLite columns are required for the MVP registry foundation. Existing session lineage stores concrete tool permission atoms; implementation should validate those atoms as canonical `ToolID`s. Existing remote MCP OAuth tokens already live in `globaldb.mcp_auth_tokens` through `mcpauth.TokenStore`; registry work must not add token fields to tool descriptors, resources, events, or metadata. Hosted MCP bind nonces are ephemeral launch correlation values and must not be stored in `mcp_auth_tokens`; if a later design needs durable hosted-proxy credential state, it must add a separate typed table with its own lifecycle and redaction tests. If a later task needs durable queryable tool-call history beyond append-only events, it must add a typed side table such as `tool_calls` rather than placing queryable call state in a session metadata JSON blob.

508	### Side-Table vs JSON Decisions
509	
510	| Domain state | Decision | Rationale |
511	|---|---|---|
512	| Tool descriptors | Typed resource specs plus runtime descriptors | Descriptors are matchable by id/source/risk/visibility and must not live as opaque metadata blobs |
513	| Toolsets | Typed config/resource records | Toolset membership affects policy and lineage; it must be queryable and expandable deterministically |
514	| Tool call events | Append-only event payloads for MVP; typed side table only if queryable history is required | Events are the operational ledger; indexed call history would be matchable state and must not be hidden in JSON metadata |
515	| Source provenance | Structured `SourceRef` fields | Raw MCP/extension names are needed for debugging and collision handling |
516	| Input/output schemas | JSON Schema blobs | Schema contents are inherently opaque external contracts and are not AGH ownership state |
517	| Tool result structured payload | JSON payload plus typed envelope fields | Result body can be arbitrary, but status, bytes, truncation, redaction, and tool id are typed envelope fields |
518	| Policy decisions | Typed `EffectiveToolDecision` | Authorization is matchable and auditable; it cannot be a JSON bag |
519	| Availability reasons | Typed reason-code list | Operator diagnostics and tests need deterministic matching |

584	## Integration Points
585	
586	### ACP
587	
588	ACP does not impose a callable tool registry pattern. AGH must not use ACP `ToolCall.title` as a policy identity or dispatch key.
589	
590	AGH will integrate with ACP by:
591	
592	- passing the hosted AGH MCP server in ACP `mcpServers` during session creation/load when the selected agent supports MCP;
593	- preserving ACP tool-call observations as session events keyed by `toolCallId`;
594	- mapping AGH-owned tool calls back into ACP lifecycle updates where relevant;
595	- treating ACP `ToolKind` as risk/display metadata, not identity;
596	- keeping `permissions.mode` as the system/session approval ceiling.
597	
598	Current-state caveat: `internal/acp.toSDKMCPServers` currently emits stdio-only `acpsdk.McpServer` values. MVP registry work must keep hosted AGH MCP as a stdio-only injected server and must not imply remote MCP HTTP ACP parity until a later implementation adds tested HTTP conversion, redacted Authorization/header handling, and provider capability checks.
599	
600	### Hosted MCP
601	
602	MVP exposure path:
603	
604	```text
605	agent session -> ACP mcpServers -> agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce> -> UDS -> daemon Tool Registry
606	```
607	
608	The hosted MCP server lists only session-callable tools. It exposes MCP `Tool.name` equal to AGH canonical `ToolID`. It does not expose unavailable, unauthorized, or conflicted tools to the model-visible surface.
609	
610	The proxy process is an SDK-based stdio MCP server: `agh tool mcp` constructs an SDK `mcp.Server`, registers one SDK `Tool` per session-callable registry descriptor using the descriptor's exact schema bytes, and serves over `mcp.StdioTransport`. The UDS bind, session/workspace scoping, approval bridge, result limiting, and dispatch callback remain AGH-owned.
611	
612	Hosted MCP authentication:
613	
614	- On session creation/load, the daemon records a short-lived hosted MCP launch record keyed by `session_id`, `workspace_id`, an opaque non-secret `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce`, expiry, and expected AGH binary path.
615	- The `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce` is a correlation nonce, not a bearer secret and not claim-token-equivalent. It may traverse ACP `mcpServers[].args` because it is insufficient without UDS peer credentials; raw `claim_token`, remote MCP OAuth tokens, and approval tokens never traverse this path.
616	- At startup, `agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce>` performs a UDS bind RPC. The daemon accepts the bind only when the nonce matches a live launch record, the Unix-domain socket peer credentials identify the same OS user, the peer executable matches the expected AGH binary, and the record has not expired.
617	- If the platform cannot provide peer credentials or executable validation, hosted MCP binding fails closed and the session receives no hosted registry projection on that platform.
618	- The daemon binds the UDS connection to exactly one session/workspace projection and rejects any later client-supplied `session_id` or `workspace_id`.
619	- The launch record is invalidated on first successful bind, session end, proxy disconnect, or TTL expiry, whichever happens first.
620	- A foreign process calling `agh tool mcp --session <id>` without a valid nonce plus matching UDS peer credentials receives a deterministic permission error and no tool projection.
621	- Redaction tests must cover ACP payload diagnostics, process diagnostics, crash bundles, logs, SSE/events, settings output, tool results, and MCP responses. The nonce may appear in AGH-owned diagnostics as a redacted/correlatable launch id, but it must never be described as a claim-token-equivalent bearer secret.
622	
623	Hosted MCP approval bridge:
624	
625	- Hosted MCP projections include only tools that are callable without a new approval prompt or tools whose session has a live daemon-mediated approval channel.
626	- When `EffectiveToolDecision.approval_required=true` and ACP `session/request_permission` is available, `Registry.Call` derives a context with `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`, issues the ACP permission request, and blocks the MCP `tools/call` response until approved, denied, timed out, canceled, or the hosted MCP stdio/UDS connection closes.
627	- Hosted MCP request contexts must reserve a guard band above approval waiting. The SDK-facing hosted MCP request deadline must be at least `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds + 5s` so a valid daemon approval bridge wait cannot be preempted by an earlier SDK deadline.
628	- When no approval channel is available, hosted MCP hides the tool from `tools/list` if that can be determined during projection. If a call still reaches dispatch, it returns `ErrToolApprovalRequired` with reason codes `approval_required` and `approval_unreachable`.
629	- Approval timeout returns `ErrToolApprovalRequired` with `approval_required` and `approval_timed_out`. Hosted MCP proxy disconnect or stdio close cancels the derived context and returns `approval_required` plus `approval_canceled`.
630	- Hosted MCP cannot satisfy approval using client-supplied arguments. CLI/HTTP/UDS may use `approval_token`; hosted MCP must use the daemon approval bridge.
631	- Remote MCP outbound call deadlines are derived independently from backend call policy and must not inherit the hosted MCP approval-wait deadline after approval completes.
632	
633	Hosted MCP lifecycle:
634	
635	- The stdio proxy is spawned by the ACP runtime from AGH-provided `mcpServers` config and is scoped to one AGH session.
636	- After successful bind, the proxy opens a daemon-owned UDS projection stream for that bound session. The daemon pushes add/remove/update deltas for session-callable tools; the proxy translates them into SDK `Server.AddTool` / remove-or-replace operations so the SDK emits `notifications/tools/list_changed` and `tools/list` stays equal to the daemon projection.
637	- If the projection stream drops, the proxy must fail closed by closing the MCP session instead of continuing to serve a stale tool catalog.
638	- The proxy exits when stdio closes, when the session stops, or when the launch record expires before successful bind.
639	- On ACP `session/load`, the daemon mints a fresh bind nonce and provides a fresh hosted MCP entry for that resumed session.
640	- The proxy never accepts a client-supplied workspace id. The daemon derives workspace id from the bound session at projection time and dispatch time.
641	
642	### Existing MCP Config And Auth
643	
644	AGH already has an MCP server configuration and remote-auth subsystem. The Tool Registry must consume those surfaces instead of defining a parallel MCP model:
645	
646	- `internal/config/provider.go` currently defines `MCPServer`, `MCPServerTransport` (`stdio`, `http`, `sse`), and `MCPAuthConfig` for OAuth 2.1 + PKCE metadata/client settings. Token material is explicitly outside config. This TechSpec hard-cuts `sse` from the accepted post-change config surface: the only valid transport values after implementation are `stdio` and `http`.
647	- `internal/config/mcpjson.go` loads `mcp.json` sidecars using `mcpServers` or `mcp_servers`.
648	- `internal/config/mcp_resource.go` validates `mcp_server` desired-state resources.
649	- `internal/mcp/auth` owns metadata discovery, PKCE state, authorization-code exchange, refresh, redacted status, logout/revocation, and `StatusValue`.
650	- `internal/store/globaldb/global_db_mcp_auth.go` persists remote MCP OAuth tokens behind `mcpauth.TokenStore` with encryption/redaction boundaries.
651	- `internal/cli/mcp_auth.go` provides the existing agent-operable commands `agh mcp auth login`, `agh mcp auth status`, and `agh mcp auth logout`.
652	- `internal/settings` and `internal/api/contract/settings.go` already expose redacted MCP auth status for settings surfaces.
653	
654	The hosted MCP bind nonce described above is not a remote MCP OAuth token and is not a bearer credential by itself. It is a daemon-minted correlation value for AGH's local stdio proxy, validated together with UDS peer credentials and the expected AGH binary path. Remote MCP OAuth tokens remain owned by `internal/mcp/auth` and `globaldb`. Registry descriptors, events, tool results, and MCP proxy arguments must never mix those credentials or reuse one lifecycle for the other.
655	
656	Remote MCP call-through uses an `MCPCallExecutor` implemented inside `internal/mcp`. `internal/tools` depends only on the executor interface and redacted `MCPAuthStatusProvider`; it must not import `internal/mcp/auth`, open `mcpauth.TokenStore`, receive raw bearer strings, or construct Authorization headers. The executor resolves bearer material internally, applies transport-specific headers in memory for the outbound MCP request, and maps failures back to redacted registry errors.
657	
658	Implementation direction for the executor:
659	
660	- stdio MCP servers use the official SDK client with `mcp.CommandTransport`;
661	- remote HTTP MCP servers use the official SDK client with `mcp.StreamableClientTransport{DisableStandaloneSSE: true}`;
662	- `MCPCallExecutor` maintains one SDK `ClientSession` per `SourceRef` with a bounded idle TTL, recreates it after transport failure or auth-status change, and applies registry-derived per-call deadlines to `ListTools` and `CallTool`.
663	- AGH may adapt its own auth state into an SDK `auth.OAuthHandler` for remote streamable transports, but `internal/mcp/auth` remains the durable token and status authority;
664	- the thin SDK `auth.OAuthHandler` adapter must implement `TokenSource` from current AGH-owned credential state and `Authorize` by attempting at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` for refreshable `authenticated` or `expired` states; it must never bootstrap a new login flow and must return only redacted reason-mapped errors otherwise;
665	- AGH must not instantiate SDK `auth.AuthorizationCodeHandler` or any other SDK-managed OAuth flow; the only allowed SDK auth integration is a thin `auth.OAuthHandler` adapter backed by already-acquired AGH credential state.
666	- MVP external MCP descriptor freshness is on-demand, not subscription-based: the daemon refreshes upstream MCP descriptors during projection rebuilds and before a call when the cached descriptor set is missing or stale. It does not rely on long-lived upstream `notifications/tools/list_changed` subscriptions from external MCP servers in MVP.
667	- If an upstream external MCP server still delivers `notifications/tools/list_changed` during an SDK session, AGH treats it only as a local cache-invalidation hint. It must not mutate registry structure from the notification alone and must not maintain standalone notification subscriptions in MVP.
668	- Config/resource validation must reject legacy `mcp_server.transport = "sse"` with a typed error instructing operators to use `transport = "http"` for SDK-backed remote MCP.
669	- no new manual MCP message framing, request/response routing, or transport-specific retry layer should be introduced outside the SDK wrapper without a later ADR.
670	
671	External MCP-backed tool availability must derive auth diagnostics from the existing auth service:
672	
673	| `internal/mcp/auth.StatusValue` | Registry reason code | Session projection behavior |
674	|---|---|---|
675	| `unconfigured` | `mcp_auth_unconfigured` | Hide external MCP tools unless the server is public and executable support exists |
676	| `needs_login` | `mcp_auth_required` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to `agh mcp auth login <server>` |
677	| `authenticated` | none | Auth does not block availability; calls may proceed only through the daemon-owned MCP adapter after registry policy passes |
678	| `expired` | `mcp_auth_expired` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to `agh mcp auth status --refresh <server>` |
679	| `invalid` | `mcp_auth_invalid` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to logout/login repair |
680	
681	Implementation correction required before the registry consumes MCP resource catalogs: current `internal/daemon/tool_mcp_resources.go` clones MCP server records through `cloneDaemonMCPServer`, which preserves only `Name`, `Command`, `Args`, and `Env`. Registry work that depends on remote MCP resources must update that clone path and its tests to preserve `Transport`, `URL`, and `Auth`; otherwise remote MCP auth metadata will be silently dropped from tool diagnostics.
682	
683	### Extensions
684	
685	Extension-installed tools are possible and should be first-class.
686	
687	Current foundation already has:
688	
689	- `extension.toml` `resources.tools`
690	- `resources.publish.families = ["tools"]`
691	- resource projection into daemon tool records
692	- extension health/status infrastructure
693	- Host API capability checks
694	
695	This TechSpec extends that by adding manifest-authoritative backend metadata, runtime reconciliation, and executable out-of-process handlers:
696	
697	```toml
698	[resources.tools.search]
699	id = "ext__linear__search"
700	description = "Search Linear issues"
701	read_only = true
702	backend.kind = "extension_host"
703	backend.handler = "search"
704	toolsets = ["linear__read"]
705	```
706	
707	TypeScript extensions define the matching runtime handler through `@agh/extension-sdk`:
708	
709	```ts
710	extension.tool("search", {
711	  readOnly: true,
712	  inputSchema: z.object({ query: z.string() }),
713	}, async ({ input, context }) => {
714	  return { content: [{ type: "text", text: await searchLinear(input.query, context) }] };
715	});
716	```
717	
718	Go extensions define the same handler through the public Go extension SDK:
719	
720	```go
721	ext.Tool("search", aghsdk.ToolOptions{
722		ReadOnly: true,
723		InputSchema: searchInputSchema,
724	}, func(ctx context.Context, req aghsdk.ToolRequest[SearchInput]) (aghsdk.ToolResult, error) {
725		return searchLinear(ctx, req.Input)
726	})
727	```
728	
729	Extensions must not freely impersonate `agh__*` or another extension namespace. Raw manifest names remain in `SourceRef`. `extension.toml` is the source of truth; SDK registration is runtime proof that the live extension process implements the manifest-declared handler and compatible schemas.
730	
731	Execution boundaries:
732	
733	- `native_go`: full MVP dispatch through in-process daemon code compiled with AGH.
734	- `extension_host`: full MVP dispatch through out-of-process extension runtime, `tool.provider`, `provide_tools`, and `tools/call`.
735	- `mcp`: full MVP dispatch through daemon-owned MCP client adapters using existing MCP config/auth.
736	- `subprocess`: not a separate public backend kind; TypeScript and Go extension SDKs use the existing subprocess runtime behind `extension_host`.
737	- `bridge`: future bridge adapter, rejected by MVP validation unless a later TechSpec enables it.
738	
739	No in-process third-party extension handlers in MVP. A Go function authored by an extension runs in the extension's subprocess binary through the Go SDK, not inside the daemon.
740	
741	### MCP Sources
742	
743	MCP-backed tools use:
744	
745	```text
746	mcp__<server>__<tool>
747	```
748	
749	The registry must preserve raw server/tool names in `SourceRef`. Sanitization collisions fail closed and mark the candidate tool `conflicted`.
750	
751	Canonicalization contract:
752	
753	```go
754	func Canonicalize(rawServer, rawTool string) (ToolID, error)
755	```
756	
757	Rules:
758	
759	- trim surrounding ASCII whitespace from `rawServer` and `rawTool`; either empty result is invalid;
760	- lowercase ASCII letters;
761	- replace `-` and `.` with `_` inside each raw segment;
762	- reject any rune outside ASCII letters, digits, `_`, `-`, and `.` rather than transliterating or dropping it;
763	- reject any normalized segment that starts with a digit, becomes empty, or contains `__` after normalization;
764	- reject any normalized segment whose leading/trailing `_` would make the reserved `__` separator ambiguous;
765	- assemble the result as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`;
766	- if the assembled ID exceeds 64 characters, fail with `id_too_long`;
767	- if two distinct raw `(server, tool)` pairs normalize to the same assembled ID, keep raw provenance in `SourceRef` and fail closed with `conflicted_sanitized_name` at registry indexing time.
768	
769	`Canonicalize` is the only allowed raw-name to canonical-`ToolID` transform for MCP sources. `MCPCallExecutor`, MCP descriptor normalization, hosted MCP registration, and config/resource validation must all reference the same helper and share one fixture set proving byte-stable results.
770	
771	AGH-managed MCP sources in MVP are the existing validated projections, not raw file scans:
772	
773	- top-level and workspace MCP config plus global/workspace `mcp.json` sidecars;
774	- provider and agent MCP server declarations;
775	- skill MCP declarations resolved by `internal/skills.MCPResolver`, including the existing `allowed_marketplace_mcp` trust gate;
776	- extension `resources.mcp_servers` records resolved by `internal/extension/resource_publication.go`;
777	- future client-supplied ACP `mcpServers` only if a later TechSpec makes them session-scoped tool sources with explicit source trust and collision handling.
778	
779	Top-level, provider, agent, and `mcp.json` declarations use `aghconfig.MCPServer` and may carry remote `transport`, `url`, and `auth` fields. Current skill and extension MCP declarations are stdio subprocess declarations with `name`, `command`, `args`, and `env`; registry work must not infer remote OAuth support from them until their manifests are explicitly extended.
780	
781	External MCP descriptor discovery must consume existing `aghconfig.MCPServer` config/resource projections and `internal/mcp/auth` redacted status. It must not read raw config files directly, bypass strict `mcp.json` decoding, bypass skill sidecar symlink hardening, bypass marketplace MCP consent, or bypass extension resource grants.
782	
783	External MCP-backed tools are executable in the MVP only through daemon-owned MCP client adapters. They are session-callable when descriptor discovery succeeds, the source is explicitly allowed, existing MCP auth status is usable, the registry policy/approval/session/hook gates pass, and dispatch can call the remote MCP server without exposing token material. Operator surfaces still show unavailable MCP tools with deterministic diagnostics when health, auth, source, policy, collision, or schema checks fail.

1000	- `hosted_mcp_enabled=true` allows AGH to inject/offer the local hosted MCP proxy for sessions.
1001	- `bind_nonce_ttl_seconds=30` bounds the hosted MCP launch record lifetime before UDS peer-credential binding.

1003	- `external_default="disabled"` means extension/MCP/dynamic executable tools are registered and operator-visible, but not session-callable until enabled by explicit tool, toolset, source-tier, or agent grants. Built-in AGH bootstrap tools remain enabled by default subject to ACP/session policy.
1004	- `approval_timeout_seconds=120` bounds daemon-mediated approvals for hosted MCP, CLI, HTTP, and UDS calls.

1010	- `ask`
1011	- `enabled`

They require explicit policy grants by `ToolID`, toolset, source, or agent plus the effective ACP/session ceiling, approval bridge when required, session lineage, and hook revalidation.

1028	
1029	Keep the existing `tools` field but harden its meaning:
1030	
1031	- `tools`: exact canonical `ToolID`s or approved wildcard patterns.
1032	- `toolsets`: named toolset IDs.
1033	- `deny_tools`: exact IDs or patterns that always narrow permissions.
1034	
1035	Session lineage should persist concrete resolved `ToolID` atoms, not broad unresolved wildcard patterns. Child session permissions must remain subsets of parent session permissions.

1040	
1041	Allowed policy pattern forms:
1042	
1043	- exact canonical IDs, for example `agh__skill_view`;
1044	- namespace-prefix wildcards ending in `*`, for example `agh__skill_*` or `mcp__github__*`;

1048

1050	- suffix wildcards such as `*__search`;

1053	- wildcard forms that would match across a reserved `__` boundary ambiguously.

1239	### Safety Invariants
1240	
1241	1. Every AGH-owned tool call enters `internal/tools.Registry.Call`; CLI, HTTP, UDS, hosted MCP, extension, and session paths cannot bypass the registry dispatch pipeline.
1242	2. Dispatch recomputes availability and `EffectiveToolDecision` at call time, even when discovery already filtered the tool.
1243	3. ACP `permissions.mode` is always a ceiling. Registry, source, agent, session, and hook policy can narrow authority but cannot raise it above the effective ACP/session mode.
1244	4. `approve-all` skips approval prompts only for otherwise allowed tools; it does not bypass explicit denies, source grants, session lineage, conflicts, unavailable backends, or hooks.
1245	5. `approve-reads` applies only to registry-classified read-only tools. Extension/MCP read-only tools also require an explicit trusted source or per-tool/toolset/source/agent grant. Mutating, destructive, open-world, network-send, and task-write tools cannot inherit read approval by display title or ACP kind.
1246	6. Session lineage stores concrete canonical `ToolID` atoms after toolset expansion; child sessions can only receive a subset of parent concrete atoms.
1247	7. Tool ID collisions fail closed. A conflicted tool is operator-visible with reason codes and absent from session/model-visible projections.
1248	8. Extension-installed tools can become executable in MVP only when the manifest-authoritative descriptor, source policy, extension health, runtime `provide_tools` descriptor, and `tools/call` handler all agree.
1249	9. Third-party extension tool handlers never run in-process in the daemon during MVP; TypeScript and Go function handlers run inside supervised extension subprocesses.
1250	10. Hooks dispatch at the call site and cannot tail event tables, spawn parallel queues, or mutate durable ownership state outside typed hook contracts.
1251	11. Tool result limiting and redaction run before results cross CLI, HTTP, UDS, MCP, SSE, logs, memory, or event payloads.
1252	12. Raw `claim_token`, MCP auth tokens, OAuth codes, PKCE verifiers, and secret bindings never appear in tool inputs/outputs persisted or emitted by AGH-owned surfaces.
1253	13. Hosted MCP `tools/list` is a projection of `GET /api/sessions/{id}/tools`; divergence is a test failure.
1254	14. Operator-visible diagnostics are not security boundaries. Hidden session projection plus dispatch-time revalidation is the security boundary.
1255	15. Toolsets are expanded deterministically and cycle-checked before session projection; recursive expansion cannot happen lazily during dispatch.
1256	16. Hosted MCP cannot bind to a session without a daemon-minted, single-use, session-bound bind nonce plus successful UDS peer credential and AGH binary validation. The nonce is not a bearer secret, is never accepted from tool input, and its launch record is invalidated on bind/session end/disconnect or `[tools.hosted_mcp].bind_nonce_ttl_seconds`.
1257	17. Hosted MCP `tools/call` cannot pass an `approval_required` decision unless the daemon completes an ACP/session-mediated approval equivalent to CLI/HTTP/UDS approval semantics within `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`; timeout and proxy disconnect return `approval_timed_out` or `approval_canceled`.
1258	18. No `agh__task_*` tool may bypass `task.Service.ClaimNextRun`, `Spawn`, session manager terminal-state authority, or task lifecycle authority. Claim/release/complete/fail/run-start operations are excluded from MVP tools.
1259	19. External `extension_host` and `mcp` backend tools are executable only through their registered runtime handles; missing handlers, missing MCP clients, missing capabilities, source denies, auth failures, or runtime mismatches fail closed before user code or remote tools run.
1260	20. Remote MCP OAuth/PKCE credentials are owned only by `internal/mcp/auth` and its `TokenStore`; the registry may consume redacted status and call through a narrow `internal/mcp/auth`-owned interface, but cannot persist, log, refresh, revoke, or copy access/refresh tokens. Raw tokens stay out of descriptors, resources, events, API responses, CLI output, MCP responses, and tool results.
1261	21. Hosted MCP bind nonces and remote MCP OAuth tokens have separate issuers, storage, lifetimes, redaction labels, and failure codes. A `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce` is not sufficient to bind an AGH hosted MCP proxy without UDS peer credential validation, must never satisfy a remote MCP server auth check, and a remote MCP OAuth token must never bind an AGH hosted MCP proxy.
1262	22. Hosted MCP SDK `Tool` registration uses `Descriptor.input_schema` and `Descriptor.output_schema` bytes as authoritative. SDK reflection-based schema generation helpers are forbidden for AGH-hosted tools.
1263	23. AGH must not use SDK `auth.AuthorizationCodeHandler`, `auth/authorization_code.go`, or any SDK-owned token cache/refresh flow for remote MCP credentials; only a thin SDK `auth.OAuthHandler` adapter backed by `internal/mcp/auth` is allowed. Its `TokenSource` reads current AGH-owned credential state and its `Authorize` path may attempt at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh`; it must never start a new login flow.
1264	24. Hosted MCP must maintain a live daemon→proxy projection stream after bind. If the stream fails, the proxy closes the MCP session rather than serving stale tools.
1265	25. AGH-owned approval timeouts govern hosted MCP approval waits; SDK-side request deadlines must not preempt a still-valid daemon approval bridge wait. Hosted MCP request deadlines therefore include at least a 5-second guard band above `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`, while remote MCP outbound deadlines are derived independently.
1266	26. Post-change remote MCP config accepts only `stdio` and `http`. Legacy `sse` must fail validation and must not be reinterpreted implicitly.

1327	## Technical Considerations
1328	
1329	### Key Decisions
1330	
1331	Decision: Tool Registry is an AGH daemon runtime service, not an ACP registry.
1332	
1333	Rationale: ACP has no callable tool registry and relies on MCP for tool discovery.
1334	
1335	Trade-off: AGH must maintain its own registry semantics and expose them through MCP/session surfaces.
1336	
1337	Decision: Use one canonical `ToolID` with `__` namespace separators.
1338	
1339	Rationale: Avoid dotted/internal plus wire alias ambiguity and stay compatible with stricter provider naming limits.
1340	
1341	Trade-off: `agh__skill_view` is less visually elegant than `agh.skill.view`, but it prevents dual identity bugs.
1342	
1343	Decision: Built-ins execute in-process; TypeScript and Go extension tools execute out-of-process through `extension_host`.
1344	
1345	Rationale: Daemon safety and extension isolation matter more than plugin convenience.
1346	
1347	Trade-off: Extension tool latency and setup are higher, but failure containment is better and extension authors still get function-based APIs.
1348	
1349	Decision: Remote MCP-backed tools execute in MVP through daemon-owned MCP clients.
1350	
1351	Rationale: MCP config/auth already exists and the registry must not become useful only for descriptors.
1352	
1353	Trade-off: The MVP must implement stricter auth redaction and adapter tests, but operators get one coherent tool model.
1354	
1355	Decision: ACP `permissions.mode` is the approval ceiling.
1356	
1357	Rationale: Avoid contradictory policy layers and keep existing settings truthful.
1358	
1359	Trade-off: Registry policy must compute effective decisions rather than simple allow/deny flags.
1360	
1361	Decision: Operator and session projections differ.
1362	
1363	Rationale: Operators need diagnostics; models should see only callable tools.
1364	
1365	Trade-off: More projection logic, but less model confusion and fewer unsafe calls.
1366	
1367	Decision: Keep cold resource records separate from runtime handles.
1368	
1369	Rationale: Resource/projector system is good for installed metadata; executable dispatch needs function/protocol handles and live health.
1370	
1371	Trade-off: More types, but cleaner lifecycle and safer extension support.
1372	
1373	### Known Risks
1374	
1375	Risk: Mutating tools mislabeled as read-only.
1376	
1377	Mitigation: Descriptor validation, review tests, policy matrix tests, and hook/audit visibility.
1378	
1379	Risk: External tool name collisions force late breaking changes.
1380	
1381	Mitigation: Enforce canonical `ToolID` grammar and fail-closed collision handling in MVP.
1382	
1383	Risk: Hosted MCP path diverges from CLI/HTTP/UDS behavior.
1384	
1385	Mitigation: Hosted MCP proxies through UDS into the same registry dispatch path.
1386	
1387	Risk: Extension tools become visible before safe runtime execution is confirmed.
1388	
1389	Mitigation: Operator-visible only until manifest/runtime reconciliation, availability, source policy, and backend handle all pass.
1390	
1391	Risk: `approve-all` is misunderstood as "execute everything."
1392	
1393	Mitigation: Docs and UI copy must clarify it auto-approves otherwise allowed calls; it does not bypass explicit denies, source grants, availability, lineage, or hooks.
1394	
1395	Risk: Toolsets create ambiguous policy language.
1396	
1397	Mitigation: Keep `tools` and `toolsets` as separate typed fields; expand toolsets to concrete `ToolID`s for session lineage.
1398	
1399	Risk: Result payloads leak secrets or overwhelm context.
1400	
1401	Mitigation: Central result limiter, redaction metadata, digesting, and output budgets.

1404	
1405	Because AGH is greenfield alpha, the implementation should hard-cut ambiguous old tool concepts instead of adding compatibility bridges:

1408	- Remove descriptor-only MVP wording and `backend_not_implemented` behavior for `extension_host` and `mcp` tools.
1409	- Remove any public standalone `subprocess` backend in favor of `extension_host` subprocess isolation.

1411	- Remove any new public use of dotted tool IDs or separate MCP wire aliases.
1412	- Remove `internal/config/provider.go` `MCPServerTransportSSE`, the corresponding `sse` validation/examples in `internal/config/config_test.go` and `internal/config/provider_test.go`, and any docs/examples that still describe `mcp_server.transport = "sse"` as a supported runtime value.
1413	- Delete any new hand-rolled MCP protocol/session/transport implementation for hosted MCP or remote MCP call-through in favor of the official SDK wrapper inside `internal/mcp`, including locally-authored MCP message structs, ad-hoc stdio framers, or custom bearer-header injectors outside `internal/mcp/auth`.
1414	- Rewrite `internal/daemon/tool_mcp_resources.go:cloneDaemonMCPServer` so registry/resource projections preserve `Transport`, `URL`, and `Auth` instead of leaving a partial MCP clone path in tree.

1416	- Update docs, tests, CLI/API examples, and task artifacts that refer to dotted IDs or dual aliasing.

1	# ADR-011: Use The Official MCP Go SDK For Protocol And Transport
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-04-28
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	The Tool Registry TechSpec currently describes hosted MCP exposure and daemon-owned MCP call-through in a way that leaves room for AGH to implement MCP protocol/session/transport behavior manually.
14	
15	That would duplicate a large amount of MCP protocol code that now exists in the official Go SDK:
16	
17	- client/server lifecycle
18	- stdio transport
19	- streamable HTTP transport
20	- tool registration
21	- `tools/list_changed`
22	- typed `tools/call` helpers
23	- transport-level OAuth hooks
24	
25	At the same time, AGH already owns product-specific MCP boundaries that the SDK should not replace:
26	
27	- `internal/mcp/auth` durable token storage and redacted status
28	- CLI/settings/API operator surfaces
29	- registry projection, policy, approval, lineage, provenance, and redaction
30	- hosted MCP UDS bind rules and session scoping
31	
32	## Decision
33	
34	AGH will use the official Go SDK `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` as the MCP protocol and transport implementation for the Tool Registry MVP.
35	
36	Adoption boundary:
37	
38	- Hosted AGH MCP proxy uses the SDK server/session/tool APIs rather than manual MCP message handling.
39	- Daemon-owned MCP call-through uses the SDK client/session/transport APIs for stdio and streamable HTTP MCP servers.
40	- AGH keeps `internal/mcp/auth` as the durable auth owner and may adapt it to SDK interfaces where useful.
41	- AGH keeps registry semantics, policy, approval, projection, redaction, provenance, and diagnostics outside the SDK.
42	
43	AGH canonical `ToolID` remains the public identity and is passed to the SDK as MCP `Tool.name`.
44	
45	## Alternatives Considered
46	
47	### Continue with hand-rolled MCP implementation
48	
49	- **Pros**: maximum internal control and no external SDK dependency.
50	- **Cons**: duplicates already-solved protocol work, increases bug surface for lifecycle/transports/auth wiring, and delays registry delivery.
51	- **Why rejected**: the official SDK already covers the protocol layer AGH needs.
52	
53	### Replace AGH auth ownership with SDK auth
54	
55	- **Pros**: more reuse around OAuth.
56	- **Cons**: loses AGH's current durable token store, redacted status model, and operator manageability surfaces.
57	- **Why rejected**: AGH auth is a product boundary, not just a transport detail.
58	
59	### Use a third-party Go MCP package instead of the official SDK
60	
61	- **Pros**: some alternatives may have simpler APIs.
62	- **Cons**: weaker alignment with the MCP spec source of truth and less direct primary-source backing for a TechSpec decision.
63	- **Why rejected**: the official SDK is the strongest primary-source-backed choice for AGH's foundation.
64	
65	## Consequences
66	
67	### Positive
68	
69	- AGH reuses the official MCP protocol/session/transport implementation.
70	- Hosted MCP and remote MCP call-through gain better spec alignment and less custom protocol risk.
71	- The Tool Registry TechSpec can focus on AGH-specific semantics instead of rebuilding MCP itself.
72	
73	### Negative
74	
75	- AGH now depends on an evolving external SDK and must track its rough edges and version compatibility.
76	- Some AGH requirements still require wrapper code because the SDK is protocol-level, not product-level.
77	
78	### Risks
79	
80	- SDK rough edges or behavior changes could leak into AGH MCP behavior.
81	  - Mitigation: pin the module version, wrap SDK usage behind `internal/mcp`, and test hosted/stdio/HTTP behavior explicitly.
82	- AGH developers may overreach and replace auth/product boundaries with SDK defaults.
83	  - Mitigation: keep `internal/mcp/auth` authoritative in the TechSpec and tests.
84	
85	## Implementation Notes
86	
87	- Add `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` through `go get`; do not edit `go.mod` by hand.
88	- Hosted MCP stdio proxy should use `mcp.NewServer`, `server.AddTool`, and `mcp.StdioTransport`, with explicit `*mcp.Tool` values carrying descriptor-owned `InputSchema` and `OutputSchema` bytes.
89	- MCP call-through should use `mcp.NewClient` + `mcp.CommandTransport` for stdio servers and `mcp.StreamableClientTransport{DisableStandaloneSSE: true}` for remote HTTP MCP servers.
90	- Legacy AGH config value `mcp_server.transport = "sse"` is removed in this workstream rather than being silently mapped onto the SDK HTTP transport.
91	- If AGH needs SDK transport auth integration, implement an adapter from AGH auth state to `auth.OAuthHandler` rather than moving token ownership out of `internal/mcp/auth`.
92	- That adapter's `Authorize` path may attempt at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` for refreshable persisted credentials and must never start a new authorization-code login flow.

1	# ADR-010: Remote MCP Call-Through In MVP
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-04-28
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	AGH already models MCP server configuration and remote auth. The previous Tool Registry spec treated MCP-backed tools as descriptors with availability diagnostics only. The revised MVP must make MCP tools executable through the same registry dispatch path as built-in and extension-host tools.
14	
15	Remote MCP call-through must not duplicate AGH's MCP auth model or leak OAuth tokens. ACP currently converts MCP servers to stdio-only session entries, so remote MCP execution should happen inside the daemon-owned registry adapter rather than by passing remote MCP config directly through ACP.
16	
17	## Decision
18	
19	Remote/local MCP tools are executable in the Tool Registry MVP.
20	
21	The daemon owns MCP client adapters that discover/list/call MCP tools from validated MCP configuration and resource sources. The adapters consume existing MCP config, transport, auth metadata, and redacted auth status from `internal/mcp/auth`. Token material remains owned by `internal/mcp/auth` and its `TokenStore`; registry descriptors and results never copy tokens. In this workstream, remote MCP transport support is hard-cut to `stdio` and streamable `http`; legacy declarative `sse` is removed from the accepted config surface.
22	
23	The call-through contract is an `MCPCallExecutor` implemented inside `internal/mcp`. `internal/tools` may depend on that interface, but it must not import `internal/mcp/auth`, open the token store, receive raw bearer strings, or construct Authorization headers. The executor resolves bearer material internally for each outbound request and returns only normalized results plus redacted errors.
24	
25	Hosted MCP remains AGH's session exposure transport. When an agent calls `mcp__...` through the hosted MCP server, the call re-enters `internal/tools.Registry.Call`; the registry then invokes the daemon-owned MCP client adapter after policy, auth, availability, hook, schema, and result-budget checks.
26	
27	## Alternatives Considered
28	
29	### Descriptor-only MCP tools
30	
31	- **Description**: Show MCP tool descriptors and auth diagnostics, but do not call them in MVP.
32	- **Pros**: Smaller security and transport surface.
33	- **Cons**: Leaves MCP as a second-class source and fails to prove external tool execution.
34	- **Why rejected**: The accepted MVP scope includes remote MCP call-through.
35	
36	### Pass remote MCP servers directly to ACP sessions
37	
38	- **Description**: Let providers connect directly to remote MCP servers.
39	- **Pros**: Less daemon adapter work.
40	- **Cons**: ACP conversion is currently stdio-only, policy/audit is provider-dependent, and AGH cannot centrally enforce result redaction or source grants.
41	- **Why rejected**: AGH needs one daemon-owned dispatch path.
42	
43	### Duplicate MCP auth in the registry
44	
45	- **Description**: Store MCP tokens or OAuth state with tool descriptors.
46	- **Pros**: Simple adapter lookup.
47	- **Cons**: Duplicates credential ownership and increases leak risk.
48	- **Why rejected**: `internal/mcp/auth` remains the sole credential owner.
49	
50	## Consequences
51	
52	### Positive
53	
54	- MCP tools become agent-callable through the same policy, visibility, hook, telemetry, and hosted MCP surfaces as built-ins and extension-host tools.
55	- Existing MCP auth and settings diagnostics remain authoritative.
56	- AGH can enforce a consistent `ToolID` and collision policy for MCP sources.
57	
58	### Negative
59	
60	- MVP must implement daemon-side MCP discovery/call clients, transport handling, timeout behavior, auth refresh/error mapping, and redaction tests.
61	- MCP adapters add more failure states to availability and session projection.
62	
63	### Risks
64	
65	- OAuth tokens could leak through registry output. Mitigation: registry consumes only redacted status and uses narrow `internal/mcp/auth` execution interfaces for bearer material.
66	- Remote MCP call latency could block hosted MCP responses. Mitigation: explicit timeouts, cancellation, and structured backend failure errors.
67	- External MCP tool names could collide after sanitization. Mitigation: fail-closed conflict handling and operator-visible diagnostics.
68	
69	## Implementation Notes
70	
71	- Reuse `aghconfig.MCPServer`, `internal/config/mcpjson.go`, `internal/config/mcp_resource.go`, skill MCP resolution, extension MCP resources, and `internal/mcp/auth`.
72	- Add `MCPCallExecutor` tests proving bearer headers are injected only inside `internal/mcp` and never cross `internal/tools` logs, errors, events, or results.
73	- Fix resource cloning paths that currently drop `Transport`, `URL`, or `Auth` before relying on remote MCP diagnostics/calls.
74	- Add MCP adapter tests for stdio, HTTP, auth-required, expired/invalid auth, collision, timeout, cancellation, redaction, and rejection of legacy `sse` config.
75	- Hosted MCP never receives remote OAuth tokens; it receives only AGH-hosted session projection entries.
76	
77	## References
78	
79	- `internal/config/provider.go`
80	- `internal/mcp/auth`
81	- `internal/store/globaldb/global_db_mcp_auth.go`
82	- `internal/acp/client.go`
83	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_acp_tool_registry_compatibility.md`
84

1	# Analysis: Official MCP Go SDK Adoption
2	
3	## Scope
4	
5	This analysis evaluates whether the Tool Registry foundation should implement MCP protocol/server/client behavior manually inside AGH, or adopt the official Go SDK for MCP and keep AGH-specific policy/auth/manageability logic above it.
6	
7	The research uses primary sources only:
8	
9	- official repository: `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`
10	- official docs site: `go.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io`
11	- locally downloaded module source resolved by `go list -m -json github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@latest`
12	- current AGH MCP code under `internal/mcp`, `internal/config`, `internal/acp`, and daemon MCP resource publication paths
13	
14	## Executive Conclusion
15	
16	AGH should adopt the official MCP Go SDK, specifically `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`, for MCP protocol/session/transport mechanics instead of planning a hand-rolled MCP implementation in the Tool Registry workstream.
17	
18	This does **not** mean handing MCP ownership to the SDK wholesale. AGH must keep these boundaries:
19	
20	- `internal/tools` remains the registry/policy/provenance/decision owner.
21	- `internal/mcp/auth` remains the durable OAuth + redacted-status owner.
22	- `internal/daemon` remains the composition root and hosted-MCP session binder.
23	- AGH-specific `ToolID`, approval bridge, UDS peer validation, reason codes, redaction rules, and manageability surfaces remain AGH-owned.
24	
25	The SDK should replace **manual MCP protocol plumbing**, not AGH policy or AGH auth/state lifecycles.
26	
27	## What The Official SDK Already Covers
28	
29	### 1. Server and client lifecycle
30	
31	The official SDK exposes first-class `mcp.Server`, `mcp.Client`, `ServerSession`, and `ClientSession` types. This is already the exact abstraction AGH needs for:
32	
33	- the hosted AGH MCP server exposed to session runtimes;
34	- daemon-owned clients used to call external MCP servers.
35	
36	This is a better fit than a custom JSON-RPC layer because the SDK already maps MCP lifecycle behavior into those session types.
37	
38	### 2. Stdio and streamable HTTP transports
39	
40	The SDK already supports:
41	
42	- `StdioTransport` on the server side;
43	- `CommandTransport` on the client side for subprocess MCP servers;
44	- `StreamableHTTPHandler`, `StreamableServerTransport`, and `StreamableClientTransport` for streamable HTTP MCP.
45	
46	That maps directly to AGH's two main MCP paths:
47	
48	- hosted AGH MCP proxy over stdio for ACP session injection;
49	- daemon-owned MCP clients for configured stdio and remote HTTP MCP servers.
50	
51	Important scope implication for AGH: in `v1.5.0` there is one remote client transport surface, `StreamableClientTransport`, with optional standalone SSE behavior controlled by `DisableStandaloneSSE`. There is no separate first-class legacy `sse` client constructor that matches AGH's current `MCPServerTransportSSE` config value. The clean greenfield move is therefore to hard-cut declarative `transport = "sse"` from the Tool Registry workstream and keep remote MCP on `transport = "http"` backed by the SDK streamable transport.
52	
53	### 3. Tool registration, schema validation, and `tools/list_changed`
54	
55	The SDK already supports server-side tool registration and client-side tool calling:
56	
57	- `Server.AddTool` and the higher-level `mcp.AddTool(...)`
58	- `ClientSession.ListTools`
59	- `ClientSession.CallTool`
60	- automatic `notifications/tools/list_changed` when tools are added after client connection
61	
62	The higher-level `AddTool` helper also infers schemas, validates input/output, and fills structured/unstructured output correctly. That removes a large amount of otherwise error-prone manual glue in AGH's hosted MCP proxy.
63	
64	### 4. OAuth hooks for remote MCP clients
65	
66	The SDK's streamable HTTP client transport supports an `OAuthHandler` interface. That means AGH does not need to re-implement header injection or transport retry behavior for 401/403 flows in remote MCP clients.
67	
68	However, the SDK auth layer is transport-oriented and in-memory by default. It is not a replacement for AGH's current durable auth ownership model.
69	
70	## What AGH Must Keep Owning
71	
72	### 1. Durable MCP auth lifecycle
73	
74	AGH already has real MCP auth product surfaces:
75	
76	- `internal/mcp/auth`
77	- `internal/store/globaldb` token persistence
78	- `agh mcp auth login/status/logout`
79	- settings/API redacted `auth_status`
80	
81	The SDK's `auth.AuthorizationCodeHandler` and `OAuthHandler` are useful protocol helpers, but they do not replace AGH's persistent token store, operator workflows, or redacted diagnostics. The right design is:
82	
83	- keep AGH auth state authoritative;
84	- optionally adapt AGH auth state behind the SDK `OAuthHandler` interface for remote streamable HTTP MCP calls.
85	
86	### 2. Registry policy, provenance, and reason codes
87	
88	The SDK is intentionally protocol-level. It does not implement AGH-specific:
89	
90	- canonical `ToolID` policy;
91	- operator/session projection split;
92	- source trust rules;
93	- lineage subset enforcement;
94	- approval bridge semantics;
95	- AGH reason-code taxonomy;
96	- result redaction and cross-surface consistency requirements.
97	
98	Those remain in AGH's registry layer.
99	
100	### 3. Hosted MCP bind/authentication model
101	
102	AGH's hosted MCP server is not just an MCP server. It is a daemon-scoped exposure transport bound to:
103	
104	- a session,
105	- a workspace,
106	- a UDS peer,
107	- an expected AGH binary,
108	- a daemon-minted bind nonce,
109	- AGH approval semantics.
110	
111	The SDK can run the MCP protocol over stdio, but AGH still owns the pre-bind/bind rules and the UDS hop into `Registry.Call`.
112	
113	## Relevant SDK Limits / Rough Edges
114	
115	### 1. Tool-name validation is broader than AGH's `ToolID` grammar
116	
117	The SDK allows a wider tool-name grammar than AGH:
118	
119	- SDK: letters, digits, `_`, `-`, `.`, length up to 128
120	- AGH: lowercase ASCII segments separated by reserved `__`, max length 64
121	
122	This is not a blocker. AGH can keep its stricter canonical `ToolID` subset and pass it to the SDK as `Tool.name`.
123	
124	### 2. SDK auth is not the AGH operator surface
125	
126	The SDK can perform OAuth flows, but AGH needs:
127	
128	- durable storage,
129	- redacted diagnostics,
130	- CLI/settings/API manageability,
131	- explicit ownership boundaries.
132	
133	Therefore the SDK auth package should be considered optional or adapter-facing, not the new source of truth.
134	
135	### 3. Streamable resumability is not on by default
136	
137	The SDK does not enable event resumability/redelivery by default. It requires an `EventStore`.
138	
139	That matters for any future AGH remote/HTTP-hosted MCP path, but it is not a blocker for the MVP hosted stdio path.
140	
141	### 4. Stateless mode is not suitable for AGH-hosted approval flows
142	
143	The SDK's stateless streamable mode cannot perform server-initiated requests. AGH's hosted MCP approval bridge and richer session behavior should therefore stay on stateful session semantics when AGH eventually exposes HTTP-hosted MCP.
144	
145	## Recommendation
146	
147	Adopt the official SDK in these exact places:
148	
149	1. Hosted AGH MCP stdio proxy:
150	   - use `mcp.NewServer`
151	   - register session-callable registry tools as SDK `Tool`s
152	   - serve over `mcp.StdioTransport`
153	
154	2. Daemon-owned MCP clients for configured servers:
155	   - stdio MCP servers via `mcp.NewClient` + `mcp.CommandTransport`
156	   - remote MCP servers via `mcp.NewClient` + `mcp.StreamableClientTransport`
157	
158	3. Keep AGH auth as the durable owner:
159	   - `internal/mcp/auth` stays authoritative
160	   - remote HTTP MCP calls may adapt AGH auth state to SDK `auth.OAuthHandler` if useful
161	
162	4. Keep AGH registry semantics outside the SDK:
163	   - projection, policy, provenance, approval, hook dispatch, telemetry, redaction, and reason codes stay AGH-owned
164	
165	## TechSpec Implications
166	
167	The TechSpec should stop implying a hand-rolled MCP protocol server/client stack. It should instead state:
168	
169	- AGH uses the official MCP Go SDK for MCP protocol lifecycle, typed tools, and stdio/streamable transports.
170	- AGH wraps the SDK inside `internal/mcp` and `agh tool mcp`; the SDK is not exposed as the policy authority.
171	- AGH keeps durable auth in `internal/mcp/auth`.
172	- AGH canonical `ToolID` remains the hosted MCP `Tool.name`.
173	
174	## Evidence
175	
176	External primary sources:
177	
178	- `https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk`
179	- `https://go.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io/`
180	- `https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk/releases`
181	
182	Local module source:
183	
184	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/README.md`
185	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/docs/protocol.md`
186	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/docs/server.md`
187	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/docs/rough_edges.md`
188	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/mcp/protocol.go`
189	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/mcp/tool.go`
190	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/auth/client.go`
191	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk@v1.5.0/auth/authorization_code.go`

1	# ADR-002: Session Tool Exposure Path
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Context
8	
9	The Tool Registry must be agent-manageable, not only an internal daemon API. AGH manages external ACP-compatible runtimes, so it cannot assume direct control over every provider's internal model API tool loop.
10	
11	The registry still needs a model-visible path for session-callable tools such as `agh__tool_search`, `agh__skill_view`, `ext__linear__search`, and `mcp__github__create_issue`. Competitor research suggests MCP is the most portable first boundary:
12	
13	- Claude Code and other runtimes already understand MCP tools.
14	- MCP keeps tool exposure protocol-based rather than driver-specific.
15	- MCP can be backed by the same registry dispatch path as CLI, HTTP, and UDS.
16	- Runtimes without MCP can still reach AGH through CLI/UDS fallback surfaces.
17	
18	## Decision
19	
20	The MVP will expose session-callable registry tools through an AGH-hosted local MCP server plus shared CLI, HTTP, and UDS contracts.
21	
22	The daemon will own one registry contract and dispatch pipeline. Surfaces call into that same pipeline:
23	
24	- hosted MCP server for model-visible AGH, extension-host, and MCP-backed tools in runtimes that support MCP,
25	- CLI commands for operator and agent fallback use,
26	- HTTP API for web/operator clients,
27	- UDS API for local trusted clients and internal AGH tools.
28	
29	Direct driver/ACP injection can be added later as an optimization for runtimes that support it, but it is not the MVP exposure path.
30	
31	## Consequences
32	
33	Every session-callable tool must be representable as an MCP tool without losing policy, availability, hook, telemetry, source provenance, auth redaction, approval, and result-budget behavior.
34	
35	The registry contract types must be shared below all surfaces rather than copied into each transport.
36	
37	Session start should be able to attach the hosted AGH MCP server for agents whose runtime supports MCP. Agents/runtimes without MCP remain supported through CLI/UDS fallback.
38	
39	The hosted MCP server is not an execution backend. It is an exposure transport. `tools/list` returns the effective session projection, and every `tools/call` re-enters `internal/tools.Registry.Call`, which resolves `native_go`, `extension_host`, or `mcp` handles and revalidates policy at dispatch time.
40	
41	The TechSpec must define how the hosted MCP server is authorized, scoped to the session/workspace, and prevented from bypassing registry dispatch.
42	
43	Live catalog deltas can be designed as a later driver capability. The MVP can refresh the hosted MCP server's tool list and expose search/list tools through the registry.
44	
45	## Rejected Alternatives
46	
47	### CLI/UDS only
48	
49	This would be simpler and still agent-operable through terminal tools, but it would not provide native tool calls for runtimes with MCP support and would leave the main "last mile" gap partially open.
50	
51	### Direct ACP/driver injection first
52	
53	This could be cleaner for a single provider, but it is less portable and would force the first implementation into provider-specific behavior.
54	
55	### HTTP/UDS only
56	
57	This would build the management API but delay the session-visible tool surface, making the foundation less useful to autonomous agents.
58	
59	## Evidence
60	
61	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_claude-code.md`: MCP tools are adapted into the same local tool contract and refreshed dynamically.
62	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_openclaw.md`: MCP is a provider backend for plugin/bundle tools.
63	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_agh_current_state.md`: AGH already resolves MCP sidecars and has CLI/HTTP/UDS-style management surfaces elsewhere.
64	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_claude_code_ideas.md`: AGH should avoid assuming direct LLM API control while still exposing AGH-owned tools through provider-neutral surfaces.
65
